title: The Courier's Gauntlet
desc: A message needs delivering through three hostile districts. Good luck.

steps.text:
The sealed message pouch is heavy, locked, and chained to a metal cuff on your wrist. Standard UnderWorld courier protocol — the message goes where you go, and you can't abandon it without the key.

The route: through the market district, across the neutral zone near Stone Pillar, and into the administrative quarter on the far side. Three districts. Three sets of creatures who would love to know what's in the pouch.

"Don't stop. Don't answer questions. Don't make eye contact." The courier captain unlocks the cuff and chains you in. "And don't run. Running attracts attention."

options.label: Walk fast through the market district
Take the back alleys — less traffic

steps.text:
The market district is crowded, loud, and perfect cover. You blend into the foot traffic, the pouch hidden under your arm, the chain covered by your sleeve.

Someone bumps you. Pickpocket? Or just crowded? You grip the pouch tighter. Another bump. This time, a hand brushes the chain. Testing.

"Nice chain," a voice says from behind you. You don't look back. You walk faster.

The neutral zone near Stone Pillar is empty — a no-man's land where territorial boundaries create a buffer. You cross in forty seconds, feeling exposed.

The administrative quarter has guards. They check your courier seal and wave you through. The receiving clerk unlocks the cuff, takes the pouch, and stamps your delivery log.

"Fastest crossing this week," he says. "Market route?" You nod. "The back alleys are faster but more dangerous. You chose correctly."

You rub the chain marks on your wrist and accept a glass of water.

The back alleys are quiet. Too quiet. The main thoroughfares are where the honest business happens. The alleys are where everything else does.

You move quickly through narrow passages, the pouch against your chest. Your footsteps echo. Other footsteps echo too — behind you, matching your pace.

You speed up. They speed up. You turn left. They turn left.

At the next intersection, you stop and spin around. Nobody's there. The alley is empty. But the echoing footsteps continue for two more beats before stopping.

You decide to take the main roads after all. The market crowd swallows you. The following stops. The rest of the delivery is uneventful.

The receiving clerk notes the delay on your log. "Alley detour?"
"Brief one."
"Followed?"
"Maybe."
"Always are. That's why we pay couriers well."

options.label: Collect your courier pay and take the main road home
